statement: GPT-4o-mini reformats the ticket into a structured prompt while the original is stored in the DB
title: GPT-4o-mini reformats the ticket into a structured prompt while the o...
rationale: PR 15: Feature/prompt rewriting Adds an optional --rewrite-prompt step that cleans up noisy tickets (Slack threads, metadata, boilerplate) before sending them to Claude Code. GPT-4o-mini reformats the ticket into a structured prompt while the original is stored in the DB. Also fixes a CLI bug where --disallowed-tools could accidentally consume the task prompt during argument parsing. Verify Build: npm run build npm link 1. ivan --rewrite-prompt "Add a loading spinner" → see "🔄 Rewriting prompt..." before Claude runs 2. ivan "some task" --rewrite-prompt → same, flag order shouldn't matter 3. ivan --rewrite-prompt → prompts interactively, doesn't crash 4. ivan "some task" → no rewriting, unchanged behaviour Verify DB after any rewrite run: sqlite3 ~/.ivan/db.sqlite "SELECT description, original description FROM tasks ORDER BY uuid DESC LIMIT 1;" description = rewritten · original description = raw input Changed files: - src/database/migrations/014 add original description column.ts - src/database/migrations/index.ts - src/database/types.ts - src/index.ts - src/services/claude-cli-executor.ts - src/services/job-manager.ts - src/services/openai-service.ts - src/services/prompt-rewriter.ts - src/services/task-executor.ts - src/types/non-interactive-config.ts
applicability: Use this in this repository’s CLI, Claude Code, hook, and automation paths.

title: Removing the down migration
rationale: I'd recommend removing the down migration. Generally I don't like down migrations because they're destructive and too easy to accidentally run in the wrong env (less dangerous for ivan cause it's totally local)
